Tyre was a marketplace for many nations. She was known for celebrations and entertainment; this made her unwilling to consider the warnings God gave through his servants. Her merchants were like princes, living like princes. When Tyre is destroyed and desolate, the merchants should abandon her. Flee to protect yourselves, but those who are uncomfortable in one place will be so in another; for when God's judgments pursue sinners, they will overtake them. Where does all this trouble come from? It is a destruction from Almighty God. God intended to convince people that all earthly glory is empty and uncertain. Let the ruin of Tyre warn all places and people to beware of pride; for whoever exalts himself will be humbled. God will do it, who has all power in his hand; but the Chaldeans will be the means.

The destruction of Tyre was not meant to last forever. The Lord will visit Tyre with mercy. But when she is released, she will use her old ways of temptation. Love of worldly wealth is spiritual idolatry; and greed is spiritual idolatry. This instructs those who have wealth to use it in the service of God. When we remain with God in our everyday work, when we do all we can to spread the gospel, then our business and earnings are holy to the Lord, if we look to his glory. Christians should conduct business as God’s servants and use riches as his stewards.
